scope.summary: The D7 AFTER LOGOFF lane is wired and now proves clean EOF, abrupt SIGKILL control, and SIGTERM logical close against the synthetic D9 Free23 fixture.
out_of_scope: Any sibling rust-oracledb driver source change

entry_points.name: scripts/rig/verify_logoff_lane.sh
entry_points.trace: The lane drives a real oracle_query, reads the server session SID, requires that session to be visible in v$session, then compares logoff-trigger row deltas by non-SQL*Plus module attribution.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run --timeout 300 -- scripts/rig/verify_logoff_lane.sh
entry_points.trace: Passed against rust-oracledb-free/FREEPDB1: clean session 237 was live and recorded +1 logical-close row; SIGKILL session 237 was live and recorded 0 rows; SIGTERM session 237 closed logically with +1 row.

entry_points.name: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo build -p oraclemcp
entry_points.trace: Built target/debug/oraclemcp before the passing D7 lane run.

entry_points.name: cargo fmt --all -- --check && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo check -p oraclemcp-core && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo check -p oraclemcp
entry_points.trace: All scoped format/check gates passed for the code paths that make stdio EOF and SIGTERM call lane logical close.

limitations: The lane uses the existing synthetic D9 Free23 fixture in rust-oracledb-free/FREEPDB1.
The lane proves stdio clean EOF and process SIGTERM; installed service/systemd and pooled HTTP surfaces remain outside this D7 close.
The full workspace gate was not run because this shared checkout has unrelated in-flight panes.
